text.title: API Monitoring — Metrics Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Is the API missing Rate/Errors/Duration instrumentation?

conclusion.verdict: add RED metrics per endpoint with templated path label
add USE metrics per DB pool / queue / thread pool

branch.question: Is user_id, request_id, or raw path in labels?

conclusion.verdict: remove; switch to path templates and bounded labels

branch.question: Is latency tracked as a Summary?

conclusion.verdict: switch to Histogram so quantiles aggregate across instances

branch.question: Is the metric format custom (not Prometheus exposition or OTLP)?

conclusion.verdict: adopt Prometheus exposition format or OTLP; pick a TSDB

branch.question: Is the workload one-shot batch (no steady traffic)?

conclusion.verdict: use job-result records instead of RED/USE metrics
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
